title: Wasted Love
content:
What shall be done for sorrow
    With love whose race is run?
    Where help is none to borrow,
    What shall be done?

    In vain his hands have spun
    The web, or drawn the furrow:
    No rest their toil hath won.

    His task is all gone thorough,
    And fruit thereof is none:
    And who dare say to-morrow
    What shall be done?
meaning:
This evocative piece by Algernon Charles Swinburne, titled "Wasted Love", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
